goal: 把 Agent Capsule 分享页改成 Codex 原生线程预览样式

summary.title: Codex-native Agent Capsule share preview
summary.what: Updated the Cloudflare Worker share page to present encrypted capsule previews as a Codex-like transcript with a persistent For Agents restore panel, and updated Worker tests for the new shell and metadata.
summary.why: The previous share page looked like a bare command page and did not clearly serve humans or agents. The new page lets humans read the preview while exposing install, dry-run, and execute import commands for agents.
summary.user_goal: 把 Agent Capsule 分享页改成 Codex 原生线程预览样式

decisions.chose: Use a Codex-style transcript column plus a sticky For Agents command panel.
decisions.rationale: This matches the desired native-thread preview feel while keeping the agent import path visible and copyable.
rejected: Keep the earlier collapsible restore drawer as the primary agent entry

decisions.chose: Keep preview decryption client-side and render text through DOM/textContent-based paths.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the existing security model and avoids injecting decrypted transcript HTML.

acknowledged_constraints.note: The change is limited to share-page presentation and manifest metadata; import-as-new-thread behavior is unchanged.

review_notes: Validated with go test ./internal/capsule ./internal/codex, npm --prefix deploy/cloudflare-worker test, npm --prefix deploy/cloudflare-worker run check, and live Worker HTML/JSON/browser checks.
Latest deployed Worker source hash matched dbde59f28dcd8dc328a5e9569a231c1dbc7a9a0b3d4d8867a916d7287c5c3ea9 before upload.
Current-session share export was attempted and blocked by the CLI secret scan with 5 findings; it was not rerun with --unsafe-include-secrets.

architectural_claims: Worker share page remains zero-dependency HTML/CSS/JS
Preview plaintext is still decrypted in the browser using the URL fragment key
behavioral_changes: /s/:id HTML now shows a Codex-like preview and a For Agents restore panel
Visible commands are populated with the full current URL including fragment
Tool summaries render as collapsed process steps rather than full tool output
security_implications: No server-side plaintext preview storage was introduced
DOM rendering continues to avoid decrypted HTML injection
Agent commands expose the fragment only client-side from location.href
